skill.name: text-to-technical-roadmap
skill.description: Converts research text into a Mermaid technical roadmap flowchart for research proposals, experiment designs, and scientific text.
skill.summary: Transforms research proposals and experiment designs into structured Mermaid flowchart roadmaps.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 49 static findings are false positives. This is an instruction-only skill containing only Markdown documentation files. External commands findings were caused by Markdown code fence delimiters (triple backticks) misidentified as shell backticks. Weak cryptographic algorithm findings were caused by MD in Mermaid being confused with cryptographic hash algorithms. Network findings are a documentation source URL. System reconnaissance findings are generic process verbs in documentation text. No executable code, network calls, or actual security risks are present.

content.user_title: Turn Research Text into Mermaid Flowchart Roadmaps
content.value_statement: Researchers often struggle to communicate complex experimental workflows clearly. This skill converts research proposals and experiment designs into structured Mermaid flowchart roadmaps that show logical progression and dependencies.
actual_capabilities: Extracts research objectives, methods, and dependencies from scientific text
Generates Mermaid flowchart TD syntax for technical roadmaps
Structures research content into logical sequential and parallel steps
Identifies causal and dependency relationships in experimental workflows
Creates professional flowcharts from research proposals and experiment designs
Produces output limited to clean Mermaid code blocks for direct rendering
limitations: Output is limited to Mermaid flowchart syntax only
Does not generate executable research code or detailed protocols
Cannot validate scientific accuracy of the input content
Only processes descriptive text, not raw data sets or images

use_cases.title: Research Proposal Structuring
use_cases.description: Converts a research proposal into a visual roadmap showing the progression from background to methodology. Helps reviewers understand the research flow at a glance.

use_cases.title: Experiment Design Visualization
use_cases.description: Transforms experiment designs into flowcharts that clarify sequential and parallel experimental steps. Useful for planning and communicating complex protocols.

use_cases.title: Grant Application Illustration
use_cases.description: Creates professional roadmap flowcharts for grant applications to visualize project timelines and methodology. Strengthens proposals with clear visual structure.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Research Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid flowchart roadmap from this research proposal: [paste research text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple conversion of research text to a visual flowchart

prompt_templates.title: Methodology Visualization
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a flowchart showing the experimental methodology from this research text. Highlight parallel steps and dependencies clearly: [paste methodology text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed methodology visualization with dependency mapping

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Phase Research Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this multi-phase research plan into a flowchart roadmap with distinct phase groupings and decision points: [paste multi-phase research text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex multi-stage research with phase boundaries

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Study Flowchart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comparative flowchart roadmap from these two research approaches. Show decision points, alternative paths, and convergence points: [paste approach 1 text] [paste approach 2 text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparative research design with branching paths

output_examples.input: A research proposal studying the effects of a new drug compound on cancer cell lines, including cell culture preparation, dose-response assays, and data analysis.
output_examples.output:
flowchart TD
    A[Background: Cancer Cell Biology] --> B[Scientific Question: Drug Efficacy]
    B --> C[Objective: Assess Compound X]
    C --> D[Cell Culture Preparation]
    D --> E[Dose-Response Assay]
    E --> F[Data Analysis]
    F --> G[Conclusion: Efficacy Assessment]

output_examples.input: An experimental design for testing gene expression in yeast under heat, cold, and oxidative stress conditions with RNA sequencing analysis.
output_examples.output:
flowchart TD
    A[Background: Stress Response in Yeast] --> B[Question: Gene Expression Patterns]
    B --> C[Prepare Cultures]
    C --> D{Stress Conditions}
    D --> E[Heat Stress]
    D --> F[Cold Stress]
    D --> G[Oxidative Stress]
    E --> H[RNA Extraction]
    F --> H
    G --> H
    H --> I[RNA Sequencing]
    I --> J[Differential Expression Analysis]
    J --> K[Conclusion: Stress-Specific Patterns]

best_practices: Provide complete research text with clear objectives and methods for the most accurate flowchart output.
Review the generated Mermaid syntax before sharing to ensure node connections correctly represent your workflow.
Use consistent scientific terminology in the input text to produce professional and readable node labels.
anti_patterns: Do not submit raw data sets or numerical tables as input; the skill processes descriptive research text only.
Do not ask for outputs in formats other than Mermaid flowchart syntax; this skill does not support alternative diagram formats.
Do not expect the skill to add new scientific content or hypotheses beyond what is provided in the input text.

faq.question: What types of text can this skill process?
faq.answer: The skill processes research proposals, experiment designs, scientific text, and project descriptions that describe a sequence of steps or objectives.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces Mermaid flowchart TD syntax that can be rendered by any Mermaid-compatible viewer or editor.

faq.question: Can the skill edit or modify an existing flowchart?
faq.answer: No. The skill converts new text into flowcharts. It does not accept or edit existing flowchart code.

faq.question: Does the skill validate the scientific accuracy of my input?
faq.answer: No. The skill structures your content visually but does not verify scientific claims, methods, or data accuracy.

faq.question: What tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can I customize the visual style of the flowchart?
faq.answer: The skill outputs standard flowchart TD syntax. You can customize colors, styles, and formatting manually after generation.
